title: Michaels Last Dundies

jokes.text: To beginnings and endings. And to middles. The unsung heroes. And to moms. The moms of the troops.
jokes.setup: Michael starts a normal toast to beginnings and endings
jokes.punchline: Escalates to nonsensical categories including 'moms of the troops'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael Scott randomness - starts normal then spirals into absurd territory

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: To new beginnings and endings. To endings and beginnings. And in between, beginnings and endings.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: To all the moms in the room. To all the moms watching at home. To all the moms of the troops.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: To all the troops. The moms of the troops. And all the troops of the moms.

jokes.text: Colorado? Yeah. Sunshine State. Yep. Don't mess with Colorado.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Colorado
jokes.punchline: Both characters confidently state wrong facts about Colorado
jokes.explanation: Two idiots confidently wrong together - good character establishment

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Colorado is known for its famous ski slopes and Rocky Mountains.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's right. And Denver is the capital of Texas.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly. Plus, Colorado is where all the potatoes come from.

jokes.text: No, no. I don't want to end up like Sonny Bobo. Everyone I know who skis is dead.
jokes.setup: Asked about skiing
jokes.punchline: Absurd fear escalates to claiming everyone he knows who skis is dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael paranoia with escalation to impossible claim

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't ski. I have a very good reason. Everyone I know who skis is dead.

jokes.text: You know, it's funny. I actually tried to get an animal Olympics going. Really? What happened? You know, life happened.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions trying to start animal Olympics
jokes.punchline: Dismisses failure with vague 'life happened'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Michael - grandiose idea dismissed with casual resignation

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was going to start animal Olympics. Life happened.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Life happened?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, life happened.

jokes.text: What are you gonna miss most about Scranton? Oh. Wow. The mountains, where things are.
jokes.setup: Sincere question about what Michael will miss
jokes.punchline: Vague, meaningless answer about 'mountains, where things are'
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to express genuine emotion comes out as word salad

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: What will you miss most about Dunder Mifflin?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I think I'll miss the mountains. You know, where things are.

jokes.text: I'm at the bar, the bar that's located in the lobby of the hotel. I do not see you. How long have you... I've been here about, gosh, over half an hour. Okay, yeah, me, too.
jokes.setup: Two people trying to find each other at 'the bar'
jokes.punchline: They're at the same bar but too oblivious to realize it
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom setup but well-executed with good pacing

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Where are you? I'm at the bar on Fifth Street.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I'm at the bar on Fifth Street too! Where are you?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm by the jukebox!

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I'm standing right next to the jukebox!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well I don't see you.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I don't see you either. This is so weird.

jokes.text: I hear your voice in the phone. And that's... Man! And... Oh, brother. Michael Scott. Deangelo Vickers. Wow. That is insane.
jokes.setup: The realization they're talking while looking at each other
jokes.punchline: Visual comedy of two idiots slowly figuring it out
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the setup - predictable but satisfying visual moment

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Are you talking to me?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm talking to you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I thought you were on the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: No, I've been standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: I have heard that it is impossible to become addicted to vodka. That is true. That is true. Vodka is non-addictive. Most of the higher spirits are undetectable. It's because of the potato.
jokes.setup: Michael shares 'knowledge' about vodka addiction
jokes.punchline: Both confidently agree on completely false information
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of two confidently incorrect people enabling each other

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Vodka is a drink of champions. I once read that vodka was invented by a man named Vodka. True story.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Absolutely. Vodka is also known to have healing properties. My grandfather drank vodka every day and lived to be 103.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly! See, this is why we understand business. We understand vodka.

jokes.text: Are her breasts different? No panties on this one. And I think the sail was folded differently.
jokes.setup: Two grown men examining what appears to be a game or puzzle
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual observations about game pieces
jokes.explanation: Typical cringe humor but relies somewhat on shock value

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Look at the curves on this piece.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's the queen.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know what it is. I'm just saying, rawr.

jokes.text: You know what would be a great Onion headline? 'Cancer prevents cancer.' That's so Onion. I know! It's so you, but it's also so Onion.
jokes.setup: Discussion about contradictory health news
jokes.punchline: Absurd Onion headline followed by mutual admiration
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good character moment of mutual delusion

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what I saw on the news the other day? They said that chocolate is good for you. Then the next day, they said it's bad for you. Make up your minds!

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I saw something similar. One week eggs are healthy, the next week they're going to kill you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly! It's like, how are we supposed to know what to eat? I read this headline that said 'Study: Eating Nothing But Sour Cream for Three Months May Lead to Death.'

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know! I mean, who eats nothing but sour cream for three months? That's crazy. But you're a smart guy, Deangelo. You probably already knew that.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Well, I do pride myself on my nutritional knowledge.

jokes.text: Well, this is it. What do you think? She'll do. Oh, she'll do just fine.
jokes.setup: Michael showing Deangelo the office
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's underwhelming approval of what Michael is proud of
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's pride and Deangelo's lukewarm response

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This is our office. State of the art equipment, great team, really top notch operation.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So what do you think?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: It's fine.

jokes.text: We should write a movie or something. I'm serious.
jokes.setup: After their evening of bonding
jokes.punchline: Michael's delusional belief they should collaborate creatively
jokes.explanation: Very Michael - one good night leads to grandiose partnership ideas

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? We should start a production company together. I'm thinking we could do a buddy cop movie.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Michael, no.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hear me out. You're the straight man, I'm the funny one. We'd be unstoppable. We'd be like Lethal Weapon but better.

jokes.text: Do not talk to me until I have had my coffee. Until I've had my coffee, do not talk to me.
jokes.setup: Deangelo being grumpy in the morning
jokes.punchline: Redundantly repeats the same coffee demand
jokes.explanation: Generic coffee joke with unnecessary repetition

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo Vickers
dialogue.line: I need coffee. Coffee. I need it. Bring me coffee. Now. Coffee.

jokes.text: Coffee keeps me regular. That's the best time of the day. I give it about seven minutes. Seven minutes from this. Beeline. So at 8:37...
jokes.setup: Discussion about coffee's effects
jokes.punchline: TMI about bathroom habits with precise timing
jokes.explanation: Bathroom humor with character specificity but relies on grossout

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Coffee goes right through me. I drink it at 9, I'm in the bathroom by 9:15.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's TMI, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's just how my body works.

jokes.text: Nope, it's not Ashton Kutcher. It's Kevin Malone. Equally handsome, equally smart.
jokes.setup: Kevin doing his Ashton Kutcher impression
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's him with delusional comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - self-aware yet delusional, great structure

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Dude, I'm like, totally Ashton Kutcher. I'm super hot, I'm in movies, I'm on Twitter...

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Wait, that's just me. I am Ashton Kutcher.

jokes.text: The Adventure Pals by Deangelo Vickers and Michael Scott. Oh, my gosh, it's 150 pages long.
jokes.setup: References their previous night's collaboration idea
jokes.punchline: They actually wrote a massively long screenplay overnight
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Michael's tendency to take things too far

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So Pam, about that idea we came up with last night...

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah? I was thinking about it all morning.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I may have gotten a little carried away. I finished the screenplay.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You finished it? It's been like twelve hours.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I couldn't sleep. I was so inspired. It's three hundred and eighty-seven pages.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Three hundred and eighty-seven pages?!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: With a full second act that I rewrote four times. And a climax that will make you cry.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Michael, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what they said about Avatar, Pam. That's what they said.

jokes.text: As you know, one of my favorite things is fanfare for its own sake.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing the new manager
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that he loves unnecessary pomp
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's love of ceremony and self-awareness about it

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I love unnecessary pomp and circumstance. It makes me feel like a king.

jokes.text: You may call them Arizona, New Mexico, Nevada, Utah. I call them heaven.
jokes.setup: Listing southwestern states
jokes.punchline: Overwrought romantic description of desert states
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - overwrought passion for mundane things

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Arizona, New Mexico, Nevada, Utah, Colorado... the American Southwest. A land of rugged beauty, where crimson mesas kiss the endless azure sky, where ancient rock formations stand as silent sentinels to civilizations long past, where the desert sun paints the earth in shades of burnt sienna and gold. It's a place where a man can find himself, or lose himself completely. Where dreams are born and die under the vast, indifferent stars.

jokes.text: I have a peanut allergy, something I live with. It's a part of me. I've learned to cope with it.
jokes.setup: Casual mention of peanut allergy
jokes.punchline: Treating minor allergy as profound life struggle
jokes.explanation: Sets up character trait while being inherently funny about melodrama

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo Vickers
dialogue.line: I have a severe peanut allergy. It's basically a death sentence.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Deangelo, my friend, you're one of the strongest men I know. How do you do it?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo Vickers
dialogue.line: Every day is a battle. I wake up, I look in the mirror, and I say, 'Today might be the day.' Every meal is a potential funeral.

jokes.text: Did I want to be made manager? Sure. A great opportunity squandered? Absolutely. A crushing blow? Yes. Will I get over it? Mmm... No. But life goes on. Not for me.
jokes.setup: Jim's talking head about not getting the manager job
jokes.punchline: Escalating melodrama ending with contradictory statements
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure with perfect Jim deadpan delivery and quotable ending

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I didn't get the manager job. Which is fine. I mean, it stings a little bit, but... it's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm not upset. I'm actually relieved. No, wait — I'm devastated. Actually, I'm mostly just confused about how I feel.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: This is the best thing that's ever happened to me. This is the worst thing that's ever happened to me. This is... fine.

jokes.text: That story makes you sound stupid. I happen to like the hilarious high jinks that I get myself into.
jokes.setup: Michael tells embarrassing story about not recognizing Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Michael frames his stupidity as charming 'high jinks'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's inability to accept criticism

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I didn't even recognize you when you first got here. I thought you were a... I don't know, some temp or something.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: You didn't recognize me?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, but in my defense, it was high jinks. Just some high jinks.

jokes.text: I always carry around a couple of these. Just in case, 'cause, you know, sometimes you need to ID yourself.
jokes.setup: Andy pulling out business cards after identity confusion
jokes.punchline: Treating business cards as official ID for basic social situations
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment - overthinking simple social situations

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. I have business cards. Look at this. See? That's me right there. That's my face. And that's my name. So I'm definitely me.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: This is basically a government-issued ID. I could use this to open a bank account, rent a car, get on a plane...

jokes.text: Office funny guy. Always glad to have an office funny guy around. I wasn't even trying to make a joke.
jokes.setup: Deangelo labels Andy as office funny guy
jokes.punchline: Andy's sincere response that he wasn't joking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Andy's obliviousness to social cues

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: You're the office funny guy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I wasn't joking.

jokes.text: This is Jim and Pam, a.k.a. Jap.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing the couple
jokes.punchline: Terrible portmanteau that sounds like a slur
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael obliviousness to offensive implications - cringe works but relies on shock

jokes.text: What started as an affair has blossomed into a family before our eyes. Well, it wasn't... It wasn't an affair. Yes, it was.
jokes.setup: Michael describing Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: Insists on calling it an affair despite Jim's correction
jokes.explanation: Good Michael stubbornness and inappropriate framing of their relationship

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Jim and Pam are having an affair.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's not an affair, we're married.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what they all say. It's still an affair.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh, well, we just have the one, but she poops for four.
jokes.setup: Deangelo mentions having four kids
jokes.punchline: Pam's TMI comparison about baby's bowel movements
jokes.explanation: Good new parent overshare but relies somewhat on grossout humor

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Four kids? That's amazing. Yeah, our baby's bowel movements are so unpredictable. Like, sometimes it's just everywhere. It's like a Jackson Pollock painting, but brown and way less valuable.

jokes.text: Someone started off on a good foot with the new boss. Yeah, they don't ever talk about careers that were made because of unplanned pregnancies.
jokes.setup: Jim's sarcastic comment about Pam's baby poop comment
jokes.punchline: Pam's dark joke about unplanned pregnancy careers
jokes.explanation: Smart, dark humor that feels earned and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So that's what we're doing now? We're making jokes about baby poop?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Well, someone has to. It's not like unplanned pregnancies come with a career counselor.

jokes.text: Meredith has shown me her breasts on three separate occasions.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing office staff to Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal information as introduction
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriateness but relies on shock value

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Deangelo, this is Pam. She's our receptionist. Her father passed away last year. Tragic.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This is Jim. He's in sales. His parents are still together, which is nice.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And this is Dwight. He has a very complicated relationship with his mother.

jokes.text: Best right-hand man in the business, but you better buy a taser to keep him from wiping your butt.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing Dwight positively
jokes.punchline: Warns about Dwight's excessive devotion in gross terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Dwight's character through Michael's crude metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight is an exceptional employee. Loyal, dedicated, hardworking. Just be careful — if you get too close to him, he'll attach himself to you like a remora fish and start sucking your blood.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's called symbiosis.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, that's called devotion. Gross, vampiric devotion.

jokes.text: I got these at the store. Apparently it's one of the most popular brands in the United States. Chips Ahoy!
jokes.setup: Dwight presenting cookies to new boss
jokes.punchline: Presents store-bought cookies as if they're homemade with brand reveal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - earnest effort that's completely wrong-headed

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I baked these myself this morning. They're my famous beet cookies, a Schrute family recipe passed down through generations.

dialogue.speaker: New Boss
dialogue.line: Wow, these look amazing, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I used only the finest ingredients. No shortcuts.

dialogue.speaker: New Boss
dialogue.line: They taste incredible. What's your secret?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, that would be a secret if I told you. But I will say, it's all about the love you put into—

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, there's a Pepperidge Farm label on the bottom.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's... quality assurance. They check all my batches.

jokes.text: Try to keep my daily caloric intake under 1,200. Deangelo, you're going to starve to death.
jokes.setup: Deangelo mentions very low calorie diet
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt assessment of the diet's danger
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Deangelo's weight issues but fairly straightforward

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I'm on a very low calorie diet.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's dangerous.

jokes.text: So you decided to have an orgy and not invite me? Come on. I call middle.
jokes.setup: Michael sees people gathered around Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Interprets normal meeting as orgy and wants to participate
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael sexual misinterpretation but somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What is happening here? Is this... are we having an orgy? Because I want in. I'm very limber.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Michael, this is just a staff meeting.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well that's not as fun.

jokes.text: 'It's one of my favorite regions.' Did I just sound totally lame? Oh. I sounded good.
jokes.setup: Andy's talking head after talking to Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Self-consciousness followed by delusional self-assurance
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his insecurity and delusion

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I just told Deangelo that I'm a bro. And a friend. And a man of the people. Which is... which is true. I am those things.

jokes.text: Along the north rim of the Grand Canyon is a forest as lush as you've ever seen. Burned. It's lush, dummy.
jokes.setup: Deangelo sharing Grand Canyon knowledge
jokes.punchline: Michael's wrong interruption and Deangelo's mild correction
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Michael's know-it-all tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: The Grand Canyon was formed by the Colorado River over millions of years.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Actually, I think it was carved out by a giant Native American warrior with a tomahawk.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: No, it was definitely the river.

jokes.text: Hey, Deangelo, what do you think about bald people? I hate them.
jokes.setup: Kevin's innocent question about bald people
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's immediate, harsh response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and surprising dark humor - great character establishment

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Do bald people get shampoo commercials?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: No, Kevin, they don't.

jokes.text: There's no turtle, Michael. I just wanted to get you here. You know me very well, Dwight.
jokes.setup: Dwight lures Michael with promise of seeing a turtle
jokes.punchline: Reveals deception and Michael's accepting response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's predictability and acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, come quick! There's a turtle out here!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: A turtle? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Gotcha! There is no turtle. I just wanted to see if you'd fall for it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay. That's fine. That's fine.

jokes.text: Now, I'm gonna have to go online and look at turtles or else I'm gonna be off the whole day.
jokes.setup: Michael's disappointment about no turtle
jokes.punchline: Childish solution to manage disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael childishness and specific weird solution

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I really wanted a turtle. But you know what? I'm not going to let that get me down. I'm going to be positive about this.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: From now on, whenever I feel sad about not having a turtle, I'm just going to imagine that I have one. Problem solved.

jokes.text: I have been Robin to your Batman for 11 years, 11 glorious years, Michael. But at a certain point, Robin needs to become Batman. Batman scares me, Dwight.
jokes.setup: Dwight's Batman/Robin metaphor for their relationship
jokes.punchline: Michael admits Batman scares him
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor with perfect Michael response showing his limitations

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You're Batman and I'm Robin.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm Batman.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm Batman.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, you can't be Batman.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Because I'm Batman.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No. I'm Batman.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. You're Batman. But you scare me.

jokes.text: I didn't know Dwight wanted to be manager.
jokes.setup: Context of Dwight's obvious ambition for years
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan claim of ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan sarcasm about obvious situation

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I had no idea Dwight wanted to be manager.

jokes.text: And we turn back, and she has spelled out the word 'Ass' in the blocks. So we're laughing, she's grinning... It was so funny. Kids are a riot. They really do say the darndest things.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam tell story about baby spelling inappropriate word
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's dismissive, clichéd response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between parents' excitement and boss's indifference

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So our baby said the worst word the other day.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah, we have no idea where she learned it.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Kids say the darnedest things! Ha ha!

jokes.text: Try it without using your name. Dunder Mifflin. This is. Oh, yeah, I like it.
jokes.setup: Deangelo asking Erin to change phone greeting
jokes.punchline: Erin's confused half-sentence and forced agreement
jokes.explanation: Good awkward management moment with Erin's confusion

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Erin, I need you to change the phone greeting. It's too bubbly.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh, um, but I thought the bubbly was— okay, yeah, I can make it less... bubbly.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Thank you.

jokes.text: Well, you know, whatever... Yeah. Whatever you think would work. What do you... Yeah. I think a change would be nice. You could do the old way or the, you know... Whichever one you want to do.
jokes.setup: Deangelo trying to give direction about phone greeting
jokes.punchline: Complete inability to make a decision or give clear direction
jokes.explanation: Good character flaw - indecisive management style creating confusion

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: When someone calls the office, I want them to hear a warm greeting. Something like, 'Hello, you've reached Dunder Mifflin.' But maybe more energetic? Or professional? Actually, I'm thinking... casual but still business-like. You know what, forget I said that. Let's go with friendly but authoritative. Or is that contradictory? Maybe just say the name of the company first. Or the person's name. Whose name should it be? Actually, I have no idea what I want.

jokes.text: Come on, you maggot. Bye-bye, calories. Don't stop, now. No one's stopping me.
jokes.setup: Deangelo exercising aggressively in conference room
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top exercise talk including responding to himself
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Deangelo's intensity and self-talk

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo Vickers
dialogue.line: Feel the burn! Yeah! Come on, push it! You got this! That's right, keep going! Don't stop now! You're almost there! Come on, come on, come on!

jokes.text: Don't hug me. No! Get away, get away.
jokes.setup: Michael approaches sweaty Deangelo after workout
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's extreme rejection of physical contact
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment but fairly predictable reaction

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey Deangelo, you look like you've been working out. That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Don't touch me.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not—

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Don't even think about touching me.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, I wasn't going to—

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: If you touch me, I will kill you.

jokes.text: Well, the water's pretty cold. A lot of octopuses out there. Well, I've been taught by the best octopus hunter in the biz.
jokes.setup: Michael warning about job challenges with ocean metaphor
jokes.punchline: Deangelo extends the metaphor to octopus hunting
jokes.explanation: Good absurd metaphor extension showing both characters' tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, working here is like being in the ocean. You never know when a shark is going to come along and take a bite out of you.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's why you gotta jam your thumb in their eye.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Shark attack. You jam your thumb in their eye, their brain shuts down, they take a nap.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: ...Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Also, octopi. Eight arms. Four mouths. So you gotta watch out.

jokes.text: No, not officially. I saw you coming out of the bathroom earlier. Well, I guess I'm gonna go back to my cave.
jokes.setup: Phyllis and Deangelo's awkward introduction
jokes.punchline: Phyllis mentions seeing him in bathroom, he retreats awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Awkward social interaction with bathroom TMI

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I saw you in the bathroom earlier.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Oh... um... I was just... washing my hands.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Right. Well. Good day.

jokes.text: And that is what they call a meet cute.
jokes.setup: After extremely awkward interaction with Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Phyllis incorrectly labels it as romantic meet-cute
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contrast between romantic terminology and awkward reality

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: That was like a romantic meet-cute! He literally threatened to kill me, and I felt like I was in a movie.

jokes.text: What do African Americans call the... What? I don't know. Help me.
jokes.setup: Andy starts what sounds like a racist joke
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off and admits he doesn't know the punchline
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up offensive joke then subverts with Andy's incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So there's a black guy, a white guy, and a Hispanic guy walking into a bar...

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Andy, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't know where I was going with that.

jokes.text: I saw a hawk today. It was just sitting right there on the overpass, looking at me.
jokes.setup: Random conversational topic from Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Mundane observation treated as significant
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Deangelo's tendency to find meaning in nothing

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo Vickers
dialogue.line: You know what I've noticed? People always say 'break a leg' before a performance, but nobody actually breaks their leg. It's just an expression.

jokes.text: And this is Erin, and she's going to shave my face. Here we go. This is how we do it.
jokes.setup: Professional barber for Deangelo vs amateur Erin for Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's confidence despite obvious disadvantage
jokes.explanation: Good setup for physical comedy and Michael's competitive nature

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not worried. I've cut hair before.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: You're letting Erin cut your hair?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, why not? How hard can it be?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I have a professional barber.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I have Erin. And she's very... enthusiastic.

jokes.text: We sort of do more of like powwows and what-ups. God! Okay, well, powwows, then.
jokes.setup: Deangelo suggests 'rap sessions'
jokes.punchline: Jim's correction to equally awkward terminology
jokes.explanation: Good office culture clash with equally cringey alternatives

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: We're gonna have rap sessions.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I think you mean 'rap' like talking, not like Eminem.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: That's what I said.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, I think you meant 'raps' like we're gonna wrap things up. You know, 'wrap sessions.'

jokes.text: Enough about your baby, okay? I'm sorry. Well, we were just... I think she was just trying to... No, no, no, I know what you're doing. Just quit it.
jokes.setup: Pam sharing baby story
jokes.punchline: Deangelo's harsh shutdown and paranoid interpretation
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Deangelo's hostility toward parents

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: So the baby was crying, and I didn't know what to do, and Jim said maybe she was hungry, so I—

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Pam. Nobody cares. This is a workplace, not a daycare. We're trying to run a business here.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Oh, I was just...

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: And another thing. The way you were looking at me during that story—I know what you're doing. You're trying to make me jealous. Well, it's not going to work, because I don't get jealous. I get even.

jokes.text: I mark it urgent 'A,' urgent 'B,' urgent 'C,' urgent 'D.' Urgent 'A' is the most important, urgent 'D' you don't even really have to worry about.
jokes.setup: Discussion about memo communication problems
jokes.punchline: Michael's overly complex urgent classification system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael over-complication of simple problem

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, listen up. I've created a color-coded memo system. Red means urgent, blue means very urgent, and green means... it's urgent but in a fun way.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, what about yellow?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yellow means... I haven't decided yet. I'm still working on it.

jokes.text: I do think that there is a web of racism and/or jealousy that is trying to keep me down.
jokes.setup: Asked what could be improved in the office
jokes.punchline: Dwight's paranoid conspiracy theory about his treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia with specific terminology

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, for starters, I think I'm being discriminated against. Michael favors Jim and Pam, and the rest of the office is in on it. They're probably taking notes on how to make my life miserable. I've documented seventeen instances of preferential treatment just this month. My desk is positioned in a way that makes me vulnerable to attacks from behind. And I'm almost certain someone's been tampering with my stapler.

jokes.text: Deangelo's great. I love the guy. But I'm not sure he's a good fit for the office. And also, I'm not sure if I love the guy.
jokes.setup: Jim's positive assessment of Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts both points he just made
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with perfect Jim-style self-contradiction and honesty

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Deangelo's a great guy. He's really turned this place around.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Actually, no, he's terrible. This place is worse than ever.

jokes.text: I saw this thing in the news. Democrats want electric cars. Republicans don't. Right. I'm thinking, how crazy is it that we got a car debate going on in a city known for gridlock.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his political joke
jokes.punchline: Weak observational humor about D.C. traffic
jokes.explanation: Weak observational humor that Andy thinks is clever but isn't

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what nobody talks about? D.C. traffic. It's terrible. I mean, you've got all these important people in Washington, running the country, and they're all just sitting in gridlock on the Beltway like everyone else. So much for being in power, right? You can't even power your way past a merge.

jokes.text: Now that's funny. That's funny. You walk much?
jokes.setup: After rejecting Andy's joke
jokes.punchline: Random question about walking as if it's comedy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Deangelo's weird sense of humor

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Why do people walk on their feet?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I... I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Because their legs are too short to walk on.

jokes.text: You know, let's not go overboard with the redecorating. And I'm still here, so...
jokes.setup: Deangelo unpacking personal items in the office
jokes.punchline: Michael's passive-aggressive territorial reminder
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's territorial anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, Deangelo, I've been thinking about the layout of this office. And I really think the way it was before — with me at that desk — was the most efficient configuration. For morale. For productivity. For... everything.

jokes.text: Technically, it only holds about 3 quarts. A little factoid.
jokes.setup: Discussion of 10-gallon hat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's unnecessary technical correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight pedantry in inappropriate social context

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Actually, it's not called a 10-gallon hat because it holds 10 gallons. It's called that because it can hold 10 gallons of water. Well, technically 9.6 gallons, but the Spanish conquistadors rounded up.

jokes.text: I think right now, I'm going to change this supermodel back into Kevin Malone. Oh! There he is. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Michael removing Kevin's wig
jokes.punchline: Treating Kevin's normal appearance as shocking transformation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Michael's typical over-the-top reaction

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, what happened to you? You look completely different!

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What? That's just my normal hair.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You were bald a second ago. This is... this is a whole new Kevin.

jokes.text: That's hot coffee. Drink some soap. Eat some hand soap.
jokes.setup: Deangelo spills coffee on himself
jokes.punchline: Michael's absurd medical advice
jokes.explanation: Good Michael panic response with absurd logic

jokes.text: I guess this is my life now.
jokes.setup: After coffee spill and chaos
jokes.punchline: Resigned acceptance of absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to chaos with relatable resignation

jokes.text: Now you're a lame duck. Yeah, well, you're a retarded owl. And Deangelo is a fat cow.
jokes.setup: Dwight calls Michael a lame duck
jokes.punchline: Michael's nonsensical animal insult escalation
jokes.explanation: Good animal metaphor escalation with Michael's childish logic

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You're a lame duck, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, you're a... you're a stupid goose.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That doesn't even make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, you're a... a dumb penguin.

jokes.text: This curly-haired idiot used to be so fat.
jokes.setup: Michael looking at old photo of Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Cruel description with obvious hair observation
jokes.explanation: Michael's cruelty is character-appropriate but relies on cheap insults

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Look at this photo of Deangelo. He's got a full head of hair here. Now look at him. It's like he's been slowly turning into a bowling ball.

jokes.text: I didn't have 10 seconds a day. I was too busy eating.
jokes.setup: Discussion about easy exercise regimen
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-aware admission about priorities
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor with relatable excuse-making

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been thinking about starting an exercise routine. Something easy, you know? Low impact.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But then I realized - I'd rather spend that time doing literally anything else. Eating, sleeping, watching TV, talking about how I should exercise...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So that's my new exercise regimen. Thinking about exercise. It's very low impact.

jokes.text: Tapas is a cuisine for groups. Not with me. Tapas party of one.
jokes.setup: Describing his past eating habits
jokes.punchline: Sad truth about eating social food alone
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy and relatability

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I used to eat an entire rotisserie chicken by myself in my car. That's a sad truth about eating social food alone.

jokes.text: Looks like you went over the tapas with food.
jokes.setup: Deangelo's story about overeating tapas
jokes.punchline: Michael's terrible pun on 'over the top'
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael bad pun - character-appropriate but weak wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said! No, wait... Over the tapas?

jokes.text: Keep in mind, I was naked.
jokes.setup: Spiritual awakening story in desert
jokes.punchline: Unexpected detail about being naked
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect timing and economy

dialogue.speaker: DeAngelo Vickers
dialogue.line: I was in the desert, having a spiritual awakening, and I realized I was completely naked.

jokes.text: Guess how much weight I lost. Two hundred and two pounds? 203. I beat it by one. I beat it by one.
jokes.setup: Deangelo asks people to guess his weight loss
jokes.punchline: Excessive pride in beating goal by minimal amount
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Deangelo's need for validation

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Alright, everybody, I want you to guess how much weight I've lost.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Um, 15 pounds?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Nope. Higher.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: 20 pounds?

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I lost 21 pounds. Beat my goal by one pound. One pound!

jokes.text: If he can lose all that weight, then I can wear fake hair.
jokes.setup: Inspired by Deangelo's transformation story
jokes.punchline: Kevin's completely unrelated and easier 'challenge'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - inspirational story leads to absurd non-equivalent goal

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got inspired by Deangelo's story. So I decided to take on my own challenge.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I'm gonna try to go the whole day without eating.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Just kidding. I'm gonna eat two sandwiches instead of three.

jokes.text: And thank me for bringing it up.
jokes.setup: After Deangelo's inspirational weight loss speech
jokes.punchline: Michael taking credit for emotional moment he created cruelly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael selfishness - makes cruel situation about himself

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I have to say, I'm really proud of what we accomplished here today. That emotional moment? That was all me. I created that. I mean, sure, Deangelo gave the speech, but I set it up. I knew exactly what would happen. I'm like a puppet master of human emotion.

jokes.text: What recommendation? From when he recommended me to replace him. Um...
jokes.setup: Dwight asking about using existing recommendation
jokes.punchline: Reveals Michael never actually recommended him
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Michael's empty promises to Dwight

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, I'm using your recommendation letter for my job application.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's great, Dwight!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Yeah, I figured since you're my boss and mentor, it would be perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight... I never actually wrote you a recommendation letter.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What do you mean you never wrote one? I've had it for months.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You... you forged my signature?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I didn't forge anything. You told me I could use one whenever I needed it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, I never said that.

jokes.text: If you're gonna go whale watching on the East Coast, you might want to bring a magazine called West Coast Whales. Because you're not gonna see any.
jokes.setup: Discussing whale watching locations
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and silly solution to no whales
jokes.explanation: Good Deangelo character moment with oddly specific advice

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been to every whale watching location on the East Coast, and you know what I found? Nothing but disappointment and empty seas.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: So here's what you do: you take a blue paint marker, you go out to the ocean at exactly 3:47 AM on a Tuesday, you draw a giant whale on the water, and boom — whale watching solved.

jokes.text: I missed the O.J. verdict. Had to read about it in the paper like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Describing consequences of peanut allergy reaction
jokes.punchline: Treating missing TV moment as ultimate tragedy
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd priorities - historical moment reduced to TV inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: I went into anaphylactic shock. My throat closed up. I couldn't breathe. I thought I was gonna die.

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: You know what the worst part was? I missed The Office.

jokes.text: Listen, you're the one who decided to leave. Come on, he's our new boss. You know we have to do this. Okay. Well, who needs them? Looks like it's just you and me, Dwight. Correction. Just you.
jokes.setup: Office choosing sides between Michael and Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Michael's support dwindles to zero as even Dwight abandons him
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure showing Michael's complete isolation

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone, I need you on my side here. Deangelo is trying to take over, and I need your support.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: I'm with Deangelo.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oscar, come on. Really?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, Michael. But Deangelo is the future. I have to go with him.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight? You too? You're my right hand man!

jokes.text: Why did you have to be so damn good? I... I'm adequate.
jokes.setup: Deangelo praising Michael emotionally
jokes.punchline: Michael's modest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Michael's rare genuine modesty

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Michael, you are the best regional manager I have ever seen. You inspire people. You make them want to come to work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not trying to toot my own horn, but I'm a pretty good boss.

jokes.text: Get your senioritis on. It's Lake Havasu time.
jokes.setup: Telling Michael to enjoy his remaining time
jokes.punchline: Spring break reference for office situation
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor mixing high school and college party references

dialogue.speaker: Deangelo
dialogue.line: Enjoy your remaining time here, Michael. Like spring break, but the opposite.

jokes.text: I guess I've been working so hard I forgot what it's like to be hardly working.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on advice to relax
jokes.punchline: Classic wordplay reversal
jokes.explanation: Classic cliché wordplay but fits Michael's tendency toward dad jokes

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about what people say about relaxing. They tell you to 'go with the flow,' but I'm more of a 'go against the flow' kind of guy. That's how I got where I am today. Well, that and ignoring most of what my therapist says.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, that baby could be the star of a show called Babies I Don't Care About.
jokes.setup: After meeting Cece and seeming to warm up
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about his disinterest in babies
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect structure and brutal honesty - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: DeAngelo Vickers
dialogue.line: Look, I'm going to be honest with you. Babies are ruining this company. They're loud, they're messy, they cry all the time. I don't understand the appeal. I mean, what do they do? Nothing. They just sit there and drool on themselves like they're in some kind of vegetative state. And don't get me started on the smell. It's like a combination of sour milk and regret.

tmdb_overview: Michael trains Deangelo to take over his job of hosting the Dundie Awards, while Erin struggles with her dislike for her boyfriend, Gabe.
insight: Emotional weight dampens laughs as Michael's farewell Dundies ceremony lands just 74.5 despite 79 jokes.
